note: Should-trigger / should-not-trigger queries for tuning activation. This skill now handles BOTH modes, so a plain 'Best of N: 4' SHOULD trigger (single pass) and a two-pass marker SHOULD trigger (two pass). 'mode' is the expected mode for should_trigger cases. The non-triggers are genuine non-tournaments (definitions, generic 'two passes of feedback', etc.).

queries.query: write a python program for hangman game, Best of N: 4
build a CSV parser, best of n: 3
refactor this function, Best of N: 5
run a best of n on this, 5 tries
generate 4 versions and rank them, pick the best
give me 6 independent attempts at this and judge which is best
write a python program for hangman game, Two Pass Best of N: 4
build a CSV parser, 2-pass best of n: 3
refactor this function, Best of N: 5 two pass
draft a distributor pitch email, two pass best of n: 4
Two Pass Best of N: 6 - implement a token bucket rate limiter
do an iterative best-of-n: run 4, learn from them, run 4 more with the lessons, then pick the best
run best of n, take the pros and cons, feed them into a second round, then final rank
generate 4 solutions, distil what worked, regenerate with that guidance, then judge all of them
two-round best of n on this regex, 5 each round
best of n but with a refinement pass: 4 attempts, learn, 4 fresh attempts, final pick
what is iterative refinement in LLM sampling?
explain how genetic algorithms do selection and mutation
review this code and give me two passes of feedback
i made two attempts at this already, which is better?
summarise this document in two passes, skim then detail
set up a two-stage build pipeline in CI
compare Opus and Sonnet on a coding task
give me one strong solution, no tournament
